name: dev.ucp.common.identity_linking
title: Identity Linking
description: Capability schema for identity linking. Businesses declare the user-authenticated scopes they offer in a flat 'scopes' map. Each key is the OAuth scope string as it appears on the wire ('{capability}:{scope}', e.g. 'dev.ucp.shopping.order:read'). Scope presence implies authentication is required to obtain it; operations not gated by any listed scope operate at public or agent-authenticated access.
$comment: Auth mechanism: OAuth 2.0 with RFC 8414 discovery. When 'config.providers' is present, platforms can chain identity from a trusted provider via RFC 8693 token exchange + RFC 7523 JWT bearer; when absent, platforms run OAuth 2.0 against the business domain. The 'provider.type' discriminator defaults to 'oauth2' and reserves space for future non-OAuth provider mechanisms (e.g., wallet attestation). The 'config' object is open for non-breaking extension; platforms MUST ignore unrecognized fields and fall back to default OAuth 2.0 against the business domain.

scope_policy.title: Scope Policy
scope_policy.description: Per-scope policy and metadata — auth constraints (e.g. min_acr, max_token_age), declarative metadata (e.g. claims produced, consent descriptions), or any other scope-specific configuration. An empty object means user authentication is required with no additional policy. Open for non-breaking extension.

scope_token.description: OAuth scope string formed by joining a capability name and a scope name with a colon: '{capability}:{scope}', e.g. 'dev.ucp.shopping.order:read'. Capability names use reverse-DNS naming; scope names denote the permission granted, defined by each capability's spec (e.g. 'read', 'manage', 'create'). Platforms request these strings verbatim in OAuth 'scope' parameters; issued tokens carry them in the 'scope' claim.

provider.title: Identity Provider
provider.description: A trusted identity provider for delegated authentication. The 'type' discriminator defaults to 'oauth2' when absent; future versions may define additional types (e.g., 'wallet') without breaking the shape.

type.description: Provider type. Defaults to 'oauth2'. Reserved for future non-OAuth provider mechanisms.

auth_url.description: Base URL for authorization server metadata discovery. Platforms resolve metadata via RFC 8414 (/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server) with OIDC fallback (/.well-known/openid-configuration) on 404.

platform_schema.title: Identity Linking (Platform)
platform_schema.description: Platform-level identity linking capability declaration. Platforms advertise support for identity linking; no auth-specific config is required.

business_schema.title: Identity Linking (Business)
business_schema.description: Business-level identity linking configuration. Businesses declare the user-authenticated scopes they offer in 'config.scopes'.

providers.description: Map of trusted identity providers keyed by reverse-domain identifier. When present, platforms can use the Accelerated IdP Flow to obtain business-issued tokens by chaining identity from a provider, instead of running a fresh OAuth flow against the business directly. When absent, platforms run OAuth 2.0 against the business domain via RFC 8414 discovery.

scopes.description: Map of user-authenticated scopes offered by this business. Each key is an OAuth scope string formed as '{capability}:{scope}' (e.g. 'dev.ucp.shopping.order:read'). Scope presence in this map implies that obtaining the scope requires user authentication; operations not gated by any listed scope operate at public or agent-authenticated access. Each value is a per-scope policy object (empty object means no additional policy beyond user auth).
